papers.title: Consistency Models: Fast High-Quality Image Synthesis
papers.abstract: We propose consistency models that map any point on the diffusion ODE trajectory directly to the origin, enabling 1-2 step generation.

papers.title: Stable Video Diffusion: Scaling Temporal Consistency
papers.abstract: We extend image diffusion models to video generation by introducing temporal attention layers and a curriculum learning strategy.

papers.title: Flow Matching for Generative Modeling
papers.abstract: We present flow matching, a simulation-free approach to training continuous normalizing flows that outperforms diffusion on image generation.

papers.title: Rectified Flow: Straightening the Generation Path
papers.abstract: We propose rectified flow that straightens diffusion trajectories, reducing the number of function evaluations from 50+ to under 10.

properties.evidence_quote: Rectified flow reduces NFE from 50+ to under 10 while maintaining FID comparable to full diffusion.

properties.assumption: Score-based diffusion is necessary for high-quality generation
properties.evidence_quote: Flow matching achieves lower FID than consistency models without requiring adversarial training.

properties.divergence: Rectified flow achieves comparable quality in fewer steps but does not match consistency model speed at 1 step
properties.evidence_quote: At 1-step generation, consistency models still outperform rectified flow by 15% on FID.

gaps.description: Video diffusion (SVD) and flow matching approaches remain disconnected. No work combines temporal consistency from SVD with the efficiency of rectified flow for video generation.
Consistency models claim 1-2 step generation superiority, but newer flow-based methods approach similar speed with better training stability. The original claims have not been revisited.
